ddls:
CREATE TABLE "service_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service request */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the request was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the request (e.g., Completed, In Progress) */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date when the request was completed */,
  "service_request_number" TEXT /* Unique service request number */,
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of service requested (foreign key reference) */,
  "street_address" TEXT /* Street address where the service is requested */,
  "zip_code" INTEGER /* ZIP code of the service request location */,
  "x_coordinate" REAL /* X coordinate for the service request location */,
  "y_coordinate" REAL /* Y coordinate for the service request location */,
  "ward" INTEGER /* Ward where the request is located */,
  "police_district" INTEGER /* Police district for the request location */,
  "community_area" INTEGER /* Community area for the request location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the service request location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the service request location */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geospatial location in string format */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date of the request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the request */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the request (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the issue */,
  "photos" TEXT /* References to photos or attachments related to the request */,
  "estimated_completion_date" TEXT /* Estimated completion date for the request */,
  "actual_completion_date" TEXT /* Actual completion date of the request */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback provided by the resident after the request is completed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_ward FOREIGN KEY ("ward") REFERENCES wards ("ward_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_community_area FOREIGN KEY ("community_area") REFERENCES community_areas ("community_area_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_service_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_type_id") REFERENCES service_types ("service_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_police_district FOREIGN KEY ("police_district") REFERENCES police_districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_types" (
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service type */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service (e.g., Street Lights - All/Out) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service type */,
  "service_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service category (foreign key reference) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_types_service_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_category_id") REFERENCES service_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the service category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wards" (
  "ward_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ward */,
  "ward_name" TEXT /* Name of the ward */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the ward */,
  "ward_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ward manager (foreign key reference) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ward_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wards_ward_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("ward_manager_id") REFERENCES ward_managers ("ward_manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ward_managers" (
  "ward_manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ward manager */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the ward manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the ward manager */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the ward manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ward_manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "police_districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each police district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the police district */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the police district */,
  "district_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district manager (foreign key reference) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_police_districts_district_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_manager_id") REFERENCES district_managers ("district_manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "district_managers" (
  "district_manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district manager */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the district manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the district manager */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the district manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "community_areas" (
  "community_area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each community area */,
  "community_area_name" TEXT /* Name of the community area */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the community area */,
  "area_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the area manager (foreign key reference) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("community_area_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_areas_area_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_manager_id") REFERENCES area_managers ("area_manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "area_managers" (
  "area_manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each area manager */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the area manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the area manager */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the area manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_status_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the status history record */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request for which status is being recorded */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the request */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the status was updated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the status update */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments on the status update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_status_history_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_status_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "ward_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's ward (foreign key reference) */,
  "police_district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's police district (foreign key reference) */,
  "community_area_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's community area (foreign key reference) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_requests" (
  "user_request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user request association */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user submitting the request */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request submitted */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date when the request was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_requests_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request related to the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., Sent, Read, Unread) */,
  "sent_by_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who sent the notification (foreign key reference) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_sent_by_id FOREIGN KEY ("sent_by_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)
